top_signals.title: Stocks, Bonds Fall as Oil Jump Fuels Fed-Hike Bets: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: A standoff between the US and Iran lifted oil prices, dragging down stocks and bonds after clashes over the Strait of Hormuz raised concerns about a disruption in energy supplies that could fuel inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fees for seas: a history of taxing waterways
top_signals.summary: From Ottoman sultans to Danish kings, Iran’s toll system for Strait of Hormuz revives an old maritime fight
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnham will struggle to exorcise Labour’s historic weakness
top_signals.summary: History shows us that the party will default to over-optimism and spending money that isn’t there
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: The deceptive promises of America’s democratic socialists
top_signals.summary: The way forward for the party is not to swing to the left

top_signals.title: UK Trade Tariff: duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas
top_signals.summary: Temporary duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas (ATQs) for importing goods into the UK.

top_signals.title: El Salvador’s Bukele set to run for third term
top_signals.summary: Authoritarian leader wins party primary unopposed

top_signals.title: Why Mastercard is playing the polite guest in the UK
top_signals.summary: The card giant is considering making a strategic exit — by selling a majority stake in payments subsidiary Vocalink
